project_privacy_data_flows.description: Files added as note attachments. Files encrypted locally via libsodium before upload to S3-compatible storage.

flow_steps.details: User selects file from device or captures via camera. File path and size retrieved.

flow_steps.code: const output = await Sodium.encryptFile(encryptionKey, { uri: ..., type: "url" });
flow_steps.details: File encrypted with libsodium (xcha-stream cipher) locally before any network transmission.
POST_NOTIFICATIONS permission requested to show upload progress notification on Android.
Encrypted file uploaded to api.notesnook.com/s3/ endpoint. Uses multipart upload for large files.
Large files uploaded via multipart upload to api.notesnook.com/s3/multipart.

project_privacy_data_flows.description: Download encrypted attachments from S3-compatible storage. Verify integrity and decrypt locally.

flow_steps.code: const downloadUrl = await resolveUrlResponse.text(); ... const request = RNFetchBlob.config({ path: tempFilePath }).fetch("GET", downloadUrl);
flow_steps.details: Resolved download URL fetched. File downloaded to temporary path in DocumentDir/.cache.
Decrypted file size compared with expected attachment size to verify integrity.

flow_steps.code: let fileUri = await Sodium.decryptFile(key, info, options?.cache ? "cache" : "file");
flow_steps.details: File decrypted locally using libsodium with stored encryption key.

flow_steps.code: export const cacheDir = Platform.OS == "ios" ? RNFetchBlob.fs.dirs.LibraryDir + "/.cache" : RNFetchBlob.fs.dirs.DocumentDir + "/.cache";
flow_steps.details: Decrypted files cached in app-specific cache directory.

project_privacy_data_flows.description: User captures photo/video from camera directly to attach to note. Uses CAMERA permission.

flow_steps.details: Camera permission declared and requested at runtime.
Camera hardware feature declared as optional (app works without camera).
Uses react-native-image-crop-picker to open camera or gallery for image selection.
User presented with image compression options before attaching. Image then encrypted and uploaded via attachment flow.
